dialogue.text: what's up chat. i made this digital puppeteering rig last week and started joking that i would give my lightning talk in front of all these brilliant people I admire as a puppet show. and then some of those people started saying,
shm. you would be remiss to not give this as a puppet show. i'm really looking forward to your puppet show.
so here we are.
I'm shm, I'm an artist and computer scientist doing HCI + AI creativity research in the Berkeley Institute of Design Lab, and sometimes places like here and here

_note: use ;/' to cycle puppets 1–7 manually within this scene

dialogue.text: I'm also Filipino.
As are many of the people annotating our data.
in crowded internet cafes and outsourced business processing warehouses.
It's been said that "There is no AI without data."
but i sometimes like to say… depending on the vibe of the room…
…that there is no AI at all.
there is the organized knowledge and creative labor of real humans, their communities – ecosystems that we systematically extract and synthesize from

there is no *A*I when the large, data-driven statistical models we call AI are, materially, cultural archives of human-made and human-managed knowledge — a kind of intelligence that is anything but artificial.

when we recognize this, it becomes evident why we are moving away from a paradigm where engineering good AI meant optimizing for values like "universal accuracy" and "efficiency" — but too often actually meant optimizing for the values of a Western, capital-driven world.
we have reached the places where building big and fast would take us. where should, and must, we reach next?

we are moving towards a paradigm where we can, and must, start designing data and models with intentionality. to be intentionally cultural. positional. contextual. artistically opinionated. relational.
and when we recognize this it becomes obvious why data scraping bots are targeting our cultural institutions — museums, libraries, archives
the intelligence isn't in here — it's in our libraries, museums, cultural institutions, and other established archives of human knowledge

Data is the material of agent design. Data is real, human intelligence, shaped into a design material. Data has come to represent, for me, how deeply we underappreciate the collective intelligence of human communities. so in my work I try to explore and design against that current.

Data is the material of agent design. Data is real, human intelligence, shaped into a design material. Data has come to represent, for me, how deeply we underappreciate the collective intelligence of human communities. so in my work I try to explore and design against that current.

Data is the material of agent design. Data is real, human intelligence, shaped into a design material. Data has come to represent, for me, how deeply we underappreciate the collective intelligence of human communities. so in my work I try to explore and design against that current.
First, a line of research being led by my mentee, Berkeley senior Sophia Liu — you would be so lucky to hire her — is motivated by something we've seen and felt as instructors here at Berkeley:
when our students feel an ounce of curiosity, an agent like ChatGPT can take them directly from question to answer.
when our students feel an ounce of curiosity, an agent like ChatGPT can take them directly from question to answer.
when our students feel an ounce of curiosity, an agent like ChatGPT can take them directly from question to answer.
This is certainly an efficient, and hopefully accurate, way to get from point A to point B.
But someone driving their own search for knowledge, such as on Wikipedia, might trace a path that looks more like this
But someone driving their own search for knowledge, such as on Wikipedia, might trace a path that looks more like this
But someone driving their own search for knowledge, such as on Wikipedia, might trace a path that looks more like this
But someone driving their own search for knowledge, such as on Wikipedia, might trace a path that looks more like this
But someone driving their own search for knowledge, such as on Wikipedia, might trace a path that looks more like this
what do we lose when we abandon hypertextual web navigation? what do we gain from authoring our own self-directed, winding paths across networks of information? Well, we give up our agency, and
we eliminate FRICTION,

_note: transparent PNG — FRICTION appears above shm's head

dialogue.text: this word is historically a kind of antivalue we've been trained as technology designers to eliminate. but in the new paradigm FRICTION represents to us a design quality that creates moments where users reflect and build relational meaning.

We're interested in tracing and designing with these kinds of cognitive journeys — these human-agency-driven creative trajectories — to preserve friction and serendipity as we move into an era of AI agents.
This is Artographer — a way of exploring art datasets as a 2.5D similarity-clustered map.
we plot AI-generated images next to their nearest neighbors in historical art space, putting new media into a relational dialogue with its cultural ancestors.
I want to emphasize that we have to do months of kind of boring public domain data labor with historical images and text to build these sorts of things.

_note: use ;/' to cycle puppets 17→18→19 (data0, data1, data2) manually

dialogue.text: and I'm continuing to build on this knowledge base, not only to support this line of research, but also because I believe doing this kind of data labor —
building newer technology and infrastructures to support older knowledge and practices — is the foundation of a healthy cultural ecosystem where AI agents and human communities can ideally

_note: transparent PNG — THRIVE appears above shm's head while both puppets held up

dialogue.text: or, at the very least, sustainably coexist.

_note: transparent PNG — SUSTAINABLY COEXIST appears above head

dialogue.text: you can see more of my work here on my website — oh my gosh there was so much i wanted to share but can't — i'll see you at the reception! thanks for listening to me yap and watching my puppet show!  -shm
